en: But God remembered Noah and all the beasts and all the livestock that were with him in the ark. And God made a wind blow over the earth, and the waters subsided.
zh: 神顧念挪亞和所有與他一同在方舟裡的走獸和牲畜； 神使風吹過大地，水就漸漸退了。

en: The fountains of the deep and the windows of the heavens were closed, the rain from the heavens was restrained,
zh: 深淵的泉源和天上的窗戶，都關閉起來；天降的大雨也止住了。

en: and the waters receded from the earth continually. At the end of 150 days the waters had abated,
zh: 水從地上不斷退去，過了一百五十天，水就消退了。

en: and in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month, the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.
zh: 七月十七日，方舟停在亞拉臘山上。

en: And the waters continued to abate until the tenth month; in the tenth month, on the first day of the month, the tops of the mountains were seen.
zh: 水繼續消退，直到十月；到了十月初一，山頂都露出來了。

en: At the end of forty days Noah opened the window of the ark that he had made
zh: 過了四十天，挪亞開了他所做的方舟的窗戶，

en: and sent forth a raven. It went to and fro until the waters were dried up from the earth.
zh: 放了一隻烏鴉出去。地上的水還沒有乾，那烏鴉就一直飛來飛去。

en: Then he sent forth a dove from him, to see if the waters had subsided from the face of the ground.
zh: 他又放了一隻鴿子出去，要看看水從地上退了沒有。

en: But the dove found no place to set her foot, and she returned to him to the ark, for the waters were still on the face of the whole earth. So he put out his hand and took her and brought her into the ark with him.
zh: 因為遍地都是水，那鴿子找不著歇腳的地方，就回到挪亞的方舟那裡；於是，挪亞伸出手去，把鴿子接進方舟裡來。

en: He waited another seven days, and again he sent forth the dove out of the ark.
zh: 他再等了七天，又把鴿子從方舟裡放出去。

en: And the dove came back to him in the evening, and behold, in her mouth was a freshly plucked olive leaf. So Noah knew that the waters had subsided from the earth.
zh: 到了黃昏的時候，鴿子回到挪亞那裡，嘴裡叼著一塊新摘下來的橄欖樹葉，挪亞就知道地上的水已經退了。

en: Then he waited another seven days and sent forth the dove, and she did not return to him anymore.
zh: 挪亞再等了七天，又把鴿子放出去，鴿子就再沒有回到他那裡。

en: In the six hundred and first year, in the first month, the first day of the month, the waters were dried from off the earth. And Noah removed the covering of the ark and looked, and behold, the face of the ground was dry.
zh: 到挪亞六百零一歲的時候，就在正月初一，地上的水都乾了；挪亞移開方舟的蓋看看，見地面已經乾了。

en: In the second month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, the earth had dried out.
zh: 到了二月二十七日，大地就都乾了。

en: Then God said to Noah,
zh: 神告訴挪亞說：

en: "Go out from the ark, you and your wife, and your sons and your sons' wives with you.
zh: “你要從方舟出來，你和你的妻子、兒子，以及兒媳都要和你一同出來。

en: Bring out with you every living thing that is with you of all flesh- birds and animals and every creeping thing that creeps on the earth- that they may swarm on the earth, and be fruitful and multiply on the earth."
zh: 所有和你在一起有生命的活物：飛鳥、牲畜和一切在地上爬行的動物，你都要帶出來，使牠們可以在地上滋生、繁殖，也可以在地上增多。”

en: So Noah went out, and his sons and his wife and his sons' wives with him.
zh: 於是，挪亞出來了，他的兒子、妻子和兒媳都與他一同出來了；

en: Every beast, every creeping thing, and every bird, everything that moves on the earth, went out by families from the ark.
zh: 各樣走獸、牲畜、飛禽和各樣在地上爬行的動物，各從自己的族類，都出了方舟。

en: Then Noah built an altar to the LORD and took some of every clean animal and some of every clean bird and offered burnt offerings on the altar.
zh: 挪亞給耶和華築了一座祭壇，拿各樣潔淨的牲畜和飛禽，獻在祭壇上作為燔祭。

en: And when the LORD smelled the pleasing aroma, the LORD said in his heart, "I will never again curse the ground because of man, for the intention of man's heart is evil from his youth. Neither will I ever again strike down every living creature as I have done.
zh: 耶和華聞了那馨香的氣味，就心裡說：“我必不再因人的緣故咒詛這地 ；我也必不再照著我作過的，擊殺各樣的活物。

en: While the earth remains, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night, shall not cease."
zh: 大地尚存之日， 播種、收割， 寒暑、 冬夏、 白晝和黑夜 必然循環不息。”
